TCA-IRR
Back to Help & Support

How to Add a New Data Source

A step-by-step guide to expanding your platform's data capabilities.

Adding and Configuring Sources
Follow these steps to connect a new external data source.
1

Navigate to the External Source Manager

From the sidebar, go to Administration > External Links. This is the central hub where all data source connections are managed.

Go to External Source Manager
2

Add a New Source

Click the 'Add Source' button at the top right of the page. This will open a form or dialog to enter the new source's details. You can also import a configuration file if you have one.

3

Configure Source Details

Fill in the necessary information for the new source, such as its Name, Category, Description, and the API URL. Accurate categorization helps with filtering.

4

Enter API Key

In the API Key field for the new source, securely paste the API key. You can use the 'Show/Hide Secrets' toggle to verify it. For free sources without a key, you can leave this blank or it may be pre-filled.

Manage All API Keys
5

Test the Connection

After entering the API key, click the 'Test Connection' (flask icon) button on the source card. This will verify that the API key is correct and that the service is reachable.

6

Activate the Source

Once the connection test is successful, toggle the 'Active' switch on the source card. This makes the data source available to the analysis modules. Only active sources will be used in evaluations.
